ruleset.name: Opinionated Rules
ruleset.description:
Opt-in checks that are deliberately NOT part of the default `csharp` ruleset because they conflict with idiomatic C#. They come straight from PHPMD's PHP-flavoured heritage, and flag patterns that are perfectly normal — often unavoidable — in C#:
* CleanCode/ElseExpression flags every `else` branch. C# uses `else` freely; early returns are encouraged but not required. * CleanCode/BooleanArgumentFlag flags every boolean parameter. The BCL is full of them, so this is an OO Single-Responsibility opinion rather than a C# convention. * CleanCode/StaticAccess flags static method calls on other classes. The BCL is organized around static classes (Math, Path, File, Convert), so in C# this is a testability opinion, not a convention. * UnusedCode/UnusedFormalParameter flags unread parameters. In C#, parameters are routinely kept to satisfy an interface, an event-handler signature (object sender, EventArgs e), or a delegate shape. * Naming/ShortVariable flags names under 3 characters. Short names are idiomatic in C# lambdas (x => x.Id), event handlers (e), and counters. * Design/CountInLoopExpression flags Count/Length in loop conditions. These are O(1) properties in .NET, so the PHP performance rationale does not transfer; flag it only if you want the stylistic discipline.
Run with: messcs <paths> <format> opinionated Or combine: messcs <paths> <format> csharp,opinionated
